quote_of_day.text:
>>46302
My jawline feels sharper and my face feels more defined since I started calorie counting and working out yaar
Wish I wasn't depressed but atleast I made progress

Looking at my arms feels good now when I touch it it feels bumpy and tight
I'm not there yet but I'll keep grinding
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of miscellaneous musings moved across the board, bringing scattered thoughts and memes. This catch-all thread drew a steady stream of comments, with Anon sharing random thoughts and observations.

A fog of uncertainty rolled in with a request from Anon seeking a lost exhibitionist video, reportedly created by someone in Delhi. The thread remained overcast, with no clear resolution in sight.

Meanwhile, a heat wave of conspiracy theories swept through the board, as Anon claimed Bollywood stars were involved with the Illuminati. The discussion was marked by a high pressure system of speculation and skepticism.

Elsewhere, a cold snap of criticism hit with Anon's claims that the Hindu community is cowardly, sparking a flurry of responses. A drizzle of gossip also continued in a thread about a cat leading incident.

articles.title: Miscellaneous musings on a catch-all thread
articles.body:
Anon opened a thread inviting others to share miscellaneous thoughts, memes, or rants that didn't warrant their own thread. The original post promised daily or every-other-day installments of such threads.

One respondent described Hyderabadi culture, highlighting its unique dialect, food, and movie recommendations. They suggested watching "The Angrez" to experience the local lingo and savored dishes like biryani, mirchi ka salan, and qubbani ka meetha.

Another poster disparaged people from Andhra Pradesh, criticizing their alleged mishandling of Hyderabad and mangling of its pronunciation. They accused these individuals of lacking historical contributions to the city, implying the Nizams built it.

Anon then mused about how access to pornography might have derailed the scientific focus of historical figures like Faraday, Maxwell, and Hertz. They claimed young men's intellects are being "killed" by excessive porn consumption.

The discussion turned to politics, with one individual questioning why Andhra Pradesh's development seemed concentrated in Hyderabad. They argued Hyderabad's growth was already well underway before certain politicians' involvement. Personal matters also surfaced.

One poster recounted a story of attempting to impress a girl from a nearby building, only to backtrack when she seemed interested. Others shared life updates, such as the benefits of calorie counting and exercise on physical appearance.

Some conversations touched on socioeconomic standing and genetics. A respondent argued addiction and scientific aptitude are largely determined by genetics, comparing contemporary issues to historical ones.

The thread also featured criticisms of Bengaluru, with one poster defending Hyderabad's cultural offerings. Not everyone was pleased with the discussion, with some lamenting insults and low-effort posts.

Ultimately, one participant wondered aloud about reducing their time on the board, opting for alternative hobbies and online activities.

articles.title: Anon Seeks Lost Exhibitionist Video
articles.body:
Coomer posted on /b/ seeking help to recover a lost video of an exhibitionist creator from Delhi.

The creator had a YouTube channel called desidivya and posted softcore content, including a 17-minute video that one anon described as a 'GOLDMINE'. Anon suggested that the creator may have removed her content after getting married.

Coomer provided a YouTube link to a related video and mentioned that the creator had an Instagram account with '10/10 banger softcore teases'.

One anon found a YouTube video of the creator wearing a bikini in a cab and described it as 'memorable'. Another anon described the creator's appearance, saying she had 'amazing tits' but was 'butterface'.

Coomer also shared a possible file path to a leaked video, and anons expressed interest in finding the lost content.

The thread included discussions of the creator's appearance and the content she posted, with some anons sharing their own experiences or reactions to her videos.

The thread remained active throughout the day, with coomer and other anons bumping the thread and expressing their continued interest in finding the lost video.

articles.title: Anon Claims Bollywood Stars Involved with Illuminati
articles.body:
Anon started a thread claiming that the Illuminati was exposed and memed out of existence by ordinary people, but suggested that they still have influence in India. The original poster mentioned that the term 'Illuminati' is overused and lacks connection to reality.

Anon countered that several Bollywood stars are involved with the Illuminati or similar groups. One claimed that Katrina Kaif was born into the 'Children Of God' cult, while her siblings managed to escape.

Another poster mentioned that Shahid Kapoor is part of Radha Swami Satsang Beas, which they described as having shady and illegal activities within its inner circles.

Other claims included Shah Rukh Khan sending satanic imagery through his film 'RAone' and Honey Singh naming one of his songs 'Satan'. Salman Khan was also accused of promoting a 'niggerlike culture' through his show 'Big Boss'.

Kamal Haasan was singled out by multiple posters as a significant figure in the Illuminati, citing examples such as the acronym of his film 'Makkal Needhi Mayyam' equalling 666, a supposed satanic symbol.

Anon also pointed out that Haasan met the Queen of England and predicted several events through his movies. The discussion also touched on the idea that the vilification and memefication of conspiracy theories could be CIA tactics to neutralize them.

articles.title: Anon discusses cat leading incident
articles.body: Anon sparked discussion with a comment about killing a cat for leading someone. Another anon described a scenario where someone might eat 'dhai bhalle for dinner', and a respondent asked if the person was alright. One anon remarked 'only in india yaaro', while another called the situation 'brutal' and a third described it as 'wholesome'. The conversation included several abusive and incomprehensible comments.

articles.title: Anon claims Hindu community is cowardly
articles.body: Anon posted that Hindus are a cowardly community, citing a video of a low-caste Sikh beating up Pakistani clerics. One respondent called Allah a 'sasty randi ka pillah' and used derogatory language towards Muslims. Another user mentioned a movie, suggesting it portrayed a low-caste narrative to appease certain groups. The conversation involved various exchanges of insults and personal attacks, with multiple users engaging in profane and offensive discussions.
